Title: KYC Flow TH
Content:
Admin Dashboard Workflow

The Admin Dashboard is an interface used by MO Agents to perform the Know Your Customer (KYC) process for merchants onboarding with Xendit. KYC is an essential verification step that helps us confirm the identity, legitimacy, and risk profile of each merchant to meet regulatory requirements and protect our platform.

This section describes the complete Admin Dashboard workflow — from initial registration from the merchant side to how MO processes the merchant’s application.

The Admin Dashboard workflow includes the following steps:

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Account Activation

1

Merchant sign up for Xendit on their own via the local dashboard

Merchant

2

MO will process Account Activation request from Admin Dashboard > Onboarding Management > Select Thailand > Tab “Merchant Ops”

MO

MO Process Application Request

3

Before proceeding with KYC, MO needs to ensure that the merchant completes or continues with their registration within six (6) months of their initial submission date.

Otherwise, the agent can proceed in rejecting the application.

MO

4

Once registration is completed, MO to select to tick on Start KYC button to proceed with KYC verification. Then, click on 'Compliance & Risk' to conduct Sanction Checks and Risk Scoring via Name and Business. Screening.

MO

5

MO needs to check the Prohibited and High Risk industries here; [Xendit Thailand] Prohibited Industries (FINAL 15MAR2024)

MO

6

For High Risk Industries, the merchant needs to undergo EDD:
Need to ask merchant to submit the following:

  • Business License - to be reviewed by TH Legal Team

  • AML/CFT Industry - to be reviewed by TH Legal Team

  • Screenshots of the onboarding procedures.

    • These screenshots should demonstrate how the license holder restricts sign-ups or applications to residents within their licensed area.

To be reviewed by TH MO based on the outcome of the business licence conditions

MO/TH Legal Team/Compliance/Country Office

7

Application needs to be forwarded to the following stakeholder for approval:

  • Compliance

  • Country office

MO

8

For Low Risk Merchants

Please proceed with the KYC for merchants based on their entity type and submitted documents.

LDD Verification: Ensure all documents are legit and accurate
BDD Verification: Ensure the website is compliant with market-specific regulations.

MO

Business Due Diligence

The Business Due Diligence (BDD) process guides MO Agents in verifying a merchant’s business information during onboarding. Agents review the merchant’s website, business description, and industry classification to ensure they align with Xendit’s policies and risk requirements.

Below are the general criteria a merchant's website must meet during the verification process:

  • The website or App must be ready, live, and accessible.

  • The website should match the nature of the business registered with Xendit

  • The website must reflect and match the contact information (such as business email, phone number, business address) registered with Xendit

  • We prefer websites and apps with catalogs or products with pricing details that are available to purchase via checkout flow.

Merchants operating in Thailand must display specific policies based on their business model to ensure legal transparency.

A. Businesses Selling Physical Goods

Agents must verify the presence of the following:

  • Shipping Policy: Clear details on delivery timelines, methods, and terms regarding delayed delivery.

  • Cancellation Policy: Terms under which a buyer can cancel an order.

  • Refund Policy: Explicit conditions for refunds. If no refunds are offered, the site must explicitly state "No Refund."

B. Service-Based Businesses

Agents must verify the presence of the following:

  • Cancellation Policy: Notice period required for cancellations and any associated penalties or refund processes.

  • Refund Policy: Conditions for eligibility and a step-by-step process for reclaiming funds post-cancellation.

If a merchant uses social media as their primary storefront and character limits prevent full disclosure, they must provide an accessible link to the following:

  • Customer Service: Phone, email, address, contact form, or an online messaging service contact.

  • Logistics & Protection: Return policy (physical goods), Refund/Dispute policy, and Cancellation policy.

  • Compliance & Legal: Legal/Export restrictions (if applicable) and specific Terms & Conditions for active promotions.

Using the Admin Dashboard, agents determine whether the merchant meets the criteria to proceed, requires resubmission or clarification, or should be declined. The steps below outline the required actions and decision points for completing the BDD process consistently:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing Business Due Dilligince

1

Agents click on merchant’s websites and do proper assessment on merchant’s bizweb, if the website passes the criteria, please mark the website as good on Admin Dashboard.

If the website does not meet the parameter or invalid/inaccessible /incomplete/etc please mark the website as bad and request resubmission to the merchant.

MO


For more information, you may read here: https://xendit.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TH/pages/edit-v2/3465085153?draftShareId=9cae6a3f-28cf-486c-8018-2afdb09d8750#The-Required-Policies-on-Merchant%E2%80%99s-Website-and-Online-Shop

Legal Due Diligence

The Legal Due Diligence (LDD) process enables MO Agents to verify the validity of legal documents submitted by merchants during onboarding. Agents review required documents in the Admin Dashboard and mark them as valid or request resubmission if invalid.

The steps below outline the standard workflow for completing LDD checks:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing Legal Due Diligence

1

For LDD, what we need to make sure is whether documents that are submitted to us are legit. Agents can click Expand All and check if the required documents are legit or not.

2

After all the documents are legit, agents can mark documents as good. If otherwise, please mark the document as bad and proceed to request documents to the merchant.

Legal Documents Required for TH

The documents required to register with Xendit vary depending on your business type. To activate your account through the dashboard, you will need to upload the relevant legal documents for verification.

As a reference, the following are sample documents that merchants are required to submit during the onboarding process:

Document

Sample

Notes

Thai ID Card

  • Front and back

Business registration

  • Must be issued by DBD (Department of Business Development)

  • Must show complete pages

  • Must be issued within 6 months (Skip this if the document has expiration date)

  • Must be signed by the authorized director(s) and have a company seal affixed on every page of documents.

BOJ5 / HS2

  • BOJ5

  • HS2

  • Must indicate the list of shareholders of the company

  • Must be issued within 6 months (Skip this if the document has expiration date)

  • Must be signed by the authorized director(s) and have a company seal affixed on every page of documents.

Business License

  1. Hotel license

  1. Clinic/ Health center/ Hospital license

  1. Insurance license

  1. Tour agency/ Tour operator

  • Must be under company’s name

  • Must be signed by the authorized director(s) and have a company seal affixed

  • Must be validated (if document stated expiry date)

Company Bank Account

  • Must be under company’s name

For more information, you may refer here: https://xendit.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TH/pages/3465085153/Merchant+Operations+-+TH?redirectedFromRestrict=true

Onboarding Decision

After completing the BDD and LDD reviews, an onboarding decision is made based on the merchant’s eligibility and verification results. This decision determines the next action required to move the merchant forward in the onboarding process.

There are three possible onboarding outcomes, each with its own workflow and follow-up steps:

Requesting Resubmission

The resubmission process is used when a merchant’s application or documents do not fully meet the required criteria but can be corrected. Agents request the merchant to update or re-upload specific information or documents through the dashboard.

This flow outlines when to request resubmission and the steps agents should follow to ensure the required updates are completed:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

1

MO mark KYC requirements as bad or good

MO

2

MO put notes on the reason why document mark as bad in the Note. Please pick on MO agent name, fill in the notes, and also check every note history when doing KYC. And then proceed to click Save.

3

Proceed to request to merchant by clicking Action and Request Resubmission

4

Please select the Request Reason. Documents marked as “Bad” will automatically appear in the list of documents for resubmission.

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant pass KYC, mark all documents & website parameters as good - as seen on image below

And then click SAVE

2

Click PASS button

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS

  • AML

  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD

Steps to Reject Merchant Application:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

After doing KYC

1

MO need to put notes on why this merchant can’t pass KYC

And then click Save

MO

2

Choose “FAIL” as KYC Decision

And then, choose Rejection Reason

Click SAVE

Business Due Diligence

The Business Due Diligence (BDD) process guides MO Agents in verifying a merchant’s business information during onboarding. Agents review the merchant’s website, business description, and industry classification to ensure they align with Xendit’s policies and risk requirements.

Below are the general criteria a merchant's website must meet during the verification process:

  • The website or App must be ready, live, and accessible.
  • The website should match the nature of the business registered with Xendit
  • The website must reflect and match the contact information (such as business email, phone number, business address) registered with Xendit
  • We prefer websites and apps with catalogs or products with pricing details that are available to purchase via checkout flow.

Merchants operating in Thailand must display specific policies based on their business model to ensure legal transparency.

A. Businesses Selling Physical Goods

Agents must verify the presence of the following:

  • Shipping Policy: Clear details on delivery timelines, methods, and terms regarding delayed delivery.
  • Cancellation Policy: Terms under which a buyer can cancel an order.
  • Refund Policy: Explicit conditions for refunds. If no refunds are offered, the site must explicitly state "No Refund."

B. Service-Based Businesses

Agents must verify the presence of the following:

  • Cancellation Policy: Notice period required for cancellations and any associated penalties or refund processes.
  • Refund Policy: Conditions for eligibility and a step-by-step process for reclaiming funds post-cancellation.

If a merchant uses social media as their primary storefront and character limits prevent full disclosure, they must provide an accessible link to the following:

  • Customer Service: Phone, email, address, contact form, or an online messaging service contact.
  • Logistics & Protection: Return policy (physical goods), Refund/Dispute policy, and Cancellation policy.
  • Compliance & Legal: Legal/Export restrictions (if applicable) and specific Terms & Conditions for active promotions.

Using the Admin Dashboard, agents determine whether the merchant meets the criteria to proceed, requires resubmission or clarification, or should be declined. The steps below outline the required actions and decision points for completing the BDD process consistently:

Category

MO Doing Business Due Dilligince

Agents click on merchant’s websites and do proper assessment on merchant’s bizweb, if the website passes the criteria, please mark the website as good on Admin Dashboard.

If the website does not meet the parameter or invalid/inaccessible /incomplete/etc please mark the website as bad and request resubmission to the merchant.

Legal Due Diligence

The Legal Due Diligence (LDD) process enables MO Agents to verify the validity of legal documents submitted by merchants during onboarding. Agents review required documents in the Admin Dashboard and mark them as valid or request resubmission if invalid.

The steps below outline the standard workflow for completing LDD checks:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing Legal Due Diligence

1

For LDD, what we need to make sure is whether documents that are submitted to us are legit. Agents can click Expand All and check if the required documents are legit or not.

2

After all the documents are legit, agents can mark documents as good. If otherwise, please mark the document as bad and proceed to request documents to the merchant.

Legal Documents Required for TH

The documents required to register with Xendit vary depending on your business type. To activate your account through the dashboard, you will need to upload the relevant legal documents for verification.

As a reference, the following are sample documents that merchants are required to submit during the onboarding process:

Document

Sample

Notes

Thai ID Card

  • Front and back

Business registration

  • Must be issued by DBD (Department of Business Development)
  • Must show complete pages
  • Must be issued within 6 months (Skip this if the document has expiration date)
  • Must be signed by the authorized director(s) and have a company seal affixed on every page of documents.

BOJ5 / HS2

  • BOJ5

  • HS2

  • Must indicate the list of shareholders of the company
  • Must be issued within 6 months (Skip this if the document has expiration date)
  • Must be signed by the authorized director(s) and have a company seal affixed on every page of documents.

Business License

  1. Hotel license

  1. Clinic/ Health center/ Hospital license

  1. Insurance license

  1. Tour agency/ Tour operator

  • Must be under company’s name
  • Must be signed by the authorized director(s) and have a company seal affixed
  • Must be validated (if document stated expiry date)

Company Bank Account

  • Must be under company’s name

For more information, you may refer here: https://xendit.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TH/pages/3465085153/Merchant+Operations+-+TH?redirectedFromRestrict=true

Onboarding Decision

After completing the BDD and LDD reviews, an onboarding decision is made based on the merchant’s eligibility and verification results. This decision determines the next action required to move the merchant forward in the onboarding process.

There are three possible onboarding outcomes, each with its own workflow and follow-up steps:

Requesting Resubmission

The resubmission process is used when a merchant’s application or documents do not fully meet the required criteria but can be corrected. Agents request the merchant to update or re-upload specific information or documents through the dashboard.

This flow outlines when to request resubmission and the steps agents should follow to ensure the required updates are completed:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

1

MO mark KYC requirements as bad or good

MO

2

MO put notes on the reason why document mark as bad in the Note. Please pick on MO agent name, fill in the notes, and also check every note history when doing KYC. And then proceed to click Save.

3

Proceed to request to merchant by clicking Action and Request Resubmission

4

Please select the Request Reason. Documents marked as “Bad” will automatically appear in the list of documents for resubmission.

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

And then click SAVE

Click PASS button

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS
  • AML
  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD
